words: This conversation with MAECI GROSCLOSE NYE took place at a home in Juliaetta,
Idaho on March 11, 1976. The interviewer is SAM SCHRAGER.
MAECI GROSECLOSE

speaker: NYE
words: Down the street there, they called it the Foster School

speaker: NYE
words: of Healing. And he had seven or eight doctors under him.

speaker: SS
words: That big?

speaker: NYE
words: Yeah. I've got the names of all of 'em down here. The doctors.

speaker: SS
words: What did the Foster School of Healing do?

speaker: NYE
words: Well, I'll tell you what they had was a cancer cure. And that really

speaker: NYE
words: worked. Because my mother lived to be ninety-four, and when she was

speaker: NYE
words: about eighty, about the age of me, she had cancers come all over her

speaker: NYE
words: face, and Doctor Foster healed every one of 'em. And that's what he

speaker: NYE
words: was, was a cancer doctor. And this town had, oh, you know, boardinghouses;

speaker: NYE
words: one on the corner there, one down the street, a big one up

speaker: NYE
words: there, and there was people from everywhere came here. Do you know

speaker: NYE
words: a old fellow that's got a store at Pullman? A big store, name of

speaker: NYE
words: 0smore? Well, his father had a store at Kamiah, and he came here

speaker: NYE
words: in early days to be doctored, him and his wife. And that's the his

speaker: NYE
words: tory of this town, having all these people here. Everybody's house

speaker: NYE
words: had somebody a boarding in it or staying.

speaker: SS
words: To be doctored?

speaker: NYE
words: Yes sir.

speaker: SS
words: Well, what do you remember about what kind of cure they had?

speaker: NYE
words: Well, he had this cancer cure. Well, course, I don't think he claim-

speaker: NYE
words: ed to cure 'em after they got really— like you know— medicine doesn't

speaker: NYE
words: cure 'em now after they get going— but when they was first come on

speaker: NYE
words: he could cure 'em. You never heard of him?

speaker: SS
words: I heard of him, but I didn't know anything about what the Foster

speaker: SS
words: School of Medicine did.

speaker: NYE
words: Well, they called it the Foster School of Healing. And he built that

speaker: NYE
words: big concrete building down there. Nothing's in it much anymore, it

speaker: NYE
words: used to be a store. And this town was a good sized town. And they

speaker: NYE
words: had all kind of buildings that went to the depot down there, on both

speaker: NYE
words: sides of the street. And then this street that went this way and

speaker: NYE
words: one that went that way.

speaker: SS
words: But so many doctors! With all those doctors they must have had an

speaker: SS
words: awful lot of people gathered here.

speaker: NYE
words: You bet they did! This town was a big town in them days. People

speaker: NYE
words: tell you it. so, but it is so.

speaker: SS
words: Besides cancer, were there cures for other illnesses too?

speaker: NYE
words: Oh, yes, yes. Doctor Foster treated all kind of ailments, it didn't

speaker: NYE
words: matter what, and all these doctors that studied under him. He had

speaker: NYE
words: his from the Palmer School of Healing somewhere in the Middle West,

speaker: NYE
words: that's what he graduated from. And I'll tell you what made him leave

speaker: NYE
words: here. This old E. W. Porter that we was talking about— well, he

speaker: NYE
words: had this little one-horse bank down here, and they moved it across

speaker: NYE
words: the street over there, that was the First Methodist Church we ever

speaker: NYE
words: had, we had in that poolhall— and, anyway, he built him a new bank

speaker: NYE
words: building on the corner. And, well, he was kinda crooked, Old Porter.

speaker: NYE
words: He got to be a bank examiner, so you know. Well, anyway, he and Fos

speaker: NYE
words: ter got into it something or other in the bank about him a stealing

speaker: NYE
words: off Foster's bank— which he did— and they got in a fight and he

speaker: NYE
words: knocked Foster down and broke his leg and the next week Foster left

speaker: NYE
words: here. He went to Clarkston and built him a beautiful building down

speaker: NYE
words: there and had his school of healing in Clarkston til he died.

speaker: SS
words: Did that some of the life of the town with him, like that?

speaker: NYE
words: Oh, it ruined it. It ruined The businesses just couldn't make

speaker: NYE
words: it so they just set a fire and burnt 'em. This whole side of this

speaker: NYE
words: street from that old church clear down there was all full of business

speaker: NYE
words: buildings, and they burnt every one of 'em. And they burnt all of

speaker: NYE
words: this side, too.

speaker: SS
words: When you say they burned, you mean, they just caught afire?

speaker: NYE
words: They just set 'em afire! They just set 'em afire! And, of course,

speaker: NYE
words: this town didn't have much water, they had that big spring on the

speaker: NYE
words: corner up there, my dad piped to town, that little round reservoir,

speaker: NYE
words: but that wasn't a drop in the bucket, you know, to— half the town

speaker: NYE
words: burned.

speaker: SS
words: Did they know who set the fire?

speaker: NYE
words: Sure, they did! They paid the insurance to 'em. I could even tell

speaker: NYE
words: you the names of 'em. (Chuckles)

speaker: SS
words: Well, you don't have to tell me. We can forget about the names.

speaker: SS
words: But what happened is what's important.

speaker: NYE
words: Well, that's what happened to 'em. They had three hotels. That old

speaker: NYE
words: building that Chuck Noble's got his store in, was the Palace Hotel,

speaker: NYE
words: that's what it was built. And they had a brickyard down here by the

speaker: NYE
words: railroad track, and they made all these bricks. And this town made

speaker: NYE
words: all these concrete blocks, too, that they built the houses out of.

speaker: SS
words: When that fire came, was it just one fire?

speaker: NYE
words: Oh, no, no. No, it was different times. Whenever the insurance got

speaker: NYE
words: too heavy, they set the fire. They just set it afire.

speaker: SS
words: And the town was built in wood and not in brick then?

speaker: NYE
words: Oh, yes. It was all wooden buildings with generally a little, you

speaker: NYE
words: know a porch out over 'em, a little kind of a sunshade built

speaker: NYE
words: out.

speaker: SS
words: They never got— Now, if I lived in town and I knew that somebody'd

speaker: SS
words: set fire and burned down part of my town, I wouldn't be too happy

speaker: SS
words: about it. I'd probably want to, you know, do something back to 'em.

speaker: NYE
words: Well, I'll tell you about my father's business. He had a nice store

speaker: NYE
words: down there, and there and there was a dance hall between

speaker: NYE
words: him and where Chuck Noble's brick building is. Well, two men had

speaker: NYE
words: this dance hall rented. It belonged to Howard HUtchison, and they

speaker: NYE
words: had a piano in there and they insured it for fifteen dollars, and

speaker: NYE
words: they set the fire and burned it and got the fifteen dollars. And

speaker: NYE
words: how I know that, my dad had a great big old iron safe in his store,

speaker: NYE
words: well, they saved the safe and this old woman that lived down on Water

speaker: NYE
words: Street, I forget what her name was— Well, anyway, she wrote a letter

speaker: NYE
words: to Ritchie, that was my dad's partner, and said, "Well, we guess we

speaker: NYE
words: got even with old Grosclose and Richardson. We burnt the place down."

speaker: NYE
words: And she signed her name to it! And they kept that letter in that safe.

speaker: NYE
words: Well, when they burnt them down, they burnt the whole block. They

speaker: NYE
words: burnt everything but Chuck . And the reason they didn't burn it, it

speaker: NYE
words: was a brick building and it was a hotel, so it didn't catch afire.

speaker: NYE
words: And there was a great big apartment building on this corner, they

speaker: NYE
words: burnt it. They burnt my dad's store; they burnt the whole block, only

speaker: NYE
words: that one building.

speaker: SS
words: What happened to your dad then? Did he lose a lot of money through

speaker: SS
words: that?

speaker: NYE
words: It just broke his heart. So then he moved his stuff in there and

speaker: NYE
words: bought that whole big building that Chuck's got his store. He had a

speaker: NYE
words: store in there. And then the upstairs, he had his undertaking bus

speaker: NYE
words: iness upstairs.

speaker: SS
words: So he had a store, too?

speaker: NYE
words: Yes. And then my mother had a millinery shop In the front part of

speaker: NYE
words: it.

speaker: SS
words: Oh. Well, did he lose a lot in there? Was he able to get anything

speaker: SS
words: from his insurance on the place?

speaker: NYE
words: I don't think so.

speaker: SS
words: That just must have been terribly rough.

speaker: NYE
words: Oh, it was awful. And, you see, this whole side over here all burned

speaker: NYE
words: but that old brick building that Doctor Bournes built. They burned

speaker: NYE
words: everything. And that Shable hall that burnt on that corner was a

speaker: NYE
words: big three story building. They lived in the bottom. They had a place

speaker: NYE
words: where they walked out on the ground, the third floor, you know. Then

speaker: NYE
words: they had this big kind of a— I don't know what kind of store it was-

speaker: NYE
words: I don't remember, but I think it was a furniture and paint. Then

speaker: NYE
words: they had the Odd Fellows Hall upstairs. Oh, yeah. This town really

speaker: NYE
words: got set afire.

speaker: SS
words: Well then, at that point, did they rebuild it in brick?

speaker: NYE
words: No. huh-uh. No, they built that church over here in concrete blocks.

speaker: NYE
words: No, they never built another brick building in this town. I don't

speaker: NYE
words: know what become of the brickyard. It was down there where the ware

speaker: NYE
words: house sets now, but then, they never had another brick building. They

speaker: NYE
words: had the concrete building, that old museum out there, they built it

speaker: NYE
words: out of concrete blocks. And they built the church and they built

speaker: NYE
words: this house, and they built that house out there on the corner that

speaker: NYE
words: Mike Hender owns. And a little house down on State Street, too. A

speaker: NYE
words: little concrete block house.

speaker: SS
words: Well, you know, when you were talking about Foster a little while ago,

speaker: SS
words: and I'm wondering— Besides being the doctor, did he take a very ac

speaker: SS
words: tive part in the town, besides that?

speaker: NYE
words: Oh, yes. My land, he was so generous with his money. It didn't mat

speaker: NYE
words: ter who wanted, he'd give it. And he had this school of healing, and

speaker: NYE
words: he had lecturers come from other towns, and then they would have these

speaker: NYE
words: meetings once a week up there and lectures. Yes, he was a really a

speaker: NYE
words: man that was— well, business minded man, and had interest in the

speaker: NYE
words: town. He really believed in it. That's how we come to come here.

speaker: NYE
words: My father come from Kentucky to Spokane, and he met Foster up there

speaker: NYE
words: accidentally, and he invited him to come here. And so, my father

speaker: NYE
words: came here.

speaker: SS
words: No kidding!

speaker: NYE
words: Yeah.

speaker: SS
words: That's really interesting.

speaker: NYE
words: Yeah, that's how he come to come here.

speaker: SS
words: That's how he found out about

speaker: NYE
words: Well, we had four trains a day, we had plenty of ways to go wherever

speaker: NYE
words: we wanted. Wasn't like today. Today you can't go anyplace. You

speaker: NYE
words: have no way to go any place.

speaker: SS
words: So, your father came, and you, came directly—

speaker: NYE
words: From Kentucky.

speaker: SS
words: And came directly down here from Spokane? When he came out here then,

speaker: SS
words: he didn't know for sure where he wanted to go? Just knew he wanted

speaker: SS
words: to settle in here some place?

speaker: NYE
words: No. No, but Foster wanted him to come here because he wanted to build

speaker: NYE
words: up this town. And so, my father went into this furniture business,

speaker: NYE
words: this store business, and then he bought out this R. H. Porter's under

speaker: NYE
words: taking business. And then, they had the telephone in there, too, for

speaker: NYE
words: all the rural, before Schupfer had it.

speaker: SS
words: What had your father done back in Kentucky?

speaker: NYE
words: Well, that's another story. My grandfather was a millionaire. And

speaker: NYE
words: we lived in Kentucky. He had oil wells. The Atlantic and Pacific

speaker: NYE
words: Oil Company. But my mother's health failed her and he made up his

speaker: NYE
words: mind that he would come West, because it might help her. And it did.

speaker: NYE
words: It cured her of everything she had and she lived to be ninety-four.

speaker: NYE
words: (Laughter)

speaker: SS
words: Well, how old was she when she came West?

speaker: NYE
words: Well, I don't know how old she was.

speaker: SS
words: Was this her father coming out with her?

speaker: NYE
words: Her father never come to—

speaker: SS
words: Oh, her husband?

speaker: NYE
words: Yes. My father. No, my grandfather died in Kentucky.

speaker: SS
words: So, he and your mother came out?

speaker: NYE
words: My father had a lot of money for those days, see, when he come here

speaker: NYE
words: to invest. But, I'll tell you Old Foster had a big interest in this

speaker: NYE
words: town. When Porter knocked him down and broke his legand the town

speaker: NYE
words: went, you know, right now.

speaker: SS
words: Well, sure, a little town to have something like that; must have been

speaker: SS
words: the biggest thing in the world for them. Bringing all that business.

speaker: NYE
words: Yeah. I've got the names of all the doctors down and I've kept track

speaker: NYE
words: of it all.

speaker: SS
words: So, with all those people— there must have been a lot of people com

speaker: SS
words: ing in from other— How far away did they come from? Did they come

speaker: SS
words: from as far away as Portland or Seattle?

speaker: NYE
words: Oh, yes. They come from every place. South Idaho, everywhere. I

speaker: NYE
words: can remember one old man, Foster talking to him, I was a kid and stood

speaker: NYE
words: around and listened to everything, you know. And he was telling him

speaker: NYE
words: about this cancer on his lip and Foster said, "Well, why didn't you

speaker: NYE
words: come before you came? We could have cured it." said, "Well, I

speaker: NYE
words: had hay to put up.

speaker: SS
words: Kay to put up?

speaker: NYE
words: See. He could have cured it if he'd a come when it first come there.

speaker: NYE
words: But it had eaten away part of his lip time he got here. Oh, my fa

speaker: NYE
words: ther buried all them, gee whiz. You know I used to help him and I

speaker: NYE
words: have nightmares yet, looking at them poar fellows. My father didn't

speaker: NYE
words: have too much education, so when he had to get this license, he had

speaker: NYE
words: to take a course, you know, in all this. Well, I did all his writing

speaker: NYE
words: and learned it all; all the undertaking business.

speaker: SS
words: You did?

speaker: NYE
words: I was twelve. (Chuckles)

speaker: SS
words: To help him?

speaker: NYE
words: Yes, and I helped him, too! And he buried the Indians, and he got

speaker: NYE
words: forty dollars for burying 'em. And he had to furnish the casket and

speaker: NYE
words: dig the grave and furnish the music and everything for forty dollars.

speaker: NYE
words: And then he had to go over to the agency at Lapwai to collect his

speaker: NYE
words: money. I always went along and furnished the music, and did the sing

speaker: NYE
words: ing and played the organ. Had them old-timer organs, you know.

speaker: SS
words: When I was a kid, I was afraid — the idea of a dead body—

speaker: NYE
words: You was?

speaker: SS
words: Well, the idea, just of course— I never had a death in my family when

speaker: SS
words: I was a kid.

speaker: NYE
words: I was raised with it. You know, when I got older, I quit going to

speaker: NYE
words: funerals. I don't care who is buried, I don't go. I just got, I

speaker: NYE
words: don t know, ^too old to have anything to do with so many.

speaker: SS
words: But when you were a kid, you didn't find it scared you?

speaker: NYE
words: Oh, no. No, it was just part of your life. Just apart of you.

speaker: SS
words: Well, how would you help your father with the bodies? Did you ac

speaker: SS
words: tually help him prepare them ?

speaker: NYE
words: Why sure. He always had what they called a cooling board. He had

speaker: NYE
words: two of 'em. And usually, they made him take 'em to the house and lay

speaker: NYE
words: 'em out in the house. But he had a room in his house down here that

speaker: NYE
words: he brought 'em home if they'd let him. And then he had these trocars

speaker: NYE
words: and these big bottles of stuff and he drained all the blood out, and

speaker: NYE
words: then he put the embalming fluid in and did body cavity work. He got

speaker: NYE
words: fifty dollars for that; if they had the fifty dollars. But you had

speaker: NYE
words: no burial permits or anything, so you didn't have to call the doctor

speaker: NYE
words: and they could kill 'em and bury 'em, and it's all right!! Nobody

speaker: NYE
words: cared. If they did, they couldn't do anything about it. But then he

speaker: NYE
words: got his license in 1910. He had to take this course; correspondence

speaker: NYE
words: course.

speaker: SS
words: Was it kind of like it is now with the time between the death and the

speaker: SS
words: burial and the services? Is it pretty much the same?

speaker: NYE
words: Yes, about the same.

speaker: SS
words: But they were less likely to use the parlor and more likely to leave

speaker: SS
words: the body at home, and have 'em decked out at home?

speaker: NYE
words: Well, some of 'em did. They wanted it left at home, so you left it

speaker: NYE
words: home. But when he moved in that old brick building down there, he had

speaker: NYE
words: several rooms up"^ for caskets, you know, on display, then he had

speaker: NYE
words: the rooms to lay 'em out, you know. And you could go there and see

speaker: NYE
words: 'em. But lots of people wouldn't let 'em move 'em from their house.

speaker: NYE
words: And sometimes you had the funeral in the house. Yeah.

speaker: SS
words: Were there many ministers here to conduct the services?

speaker: NYE
words: Oh, yes. Thiltown had churches. They had a Catholic Church, a Lu

speaker: NYE
words: theran Church, a Presbyterian Church, a Christian Church— well, I

speaker: NYE
words: don't know how many. I've got 'em down in my history.

speaker: SS
words: Quite a few.

speaker: NYE
words: Yes. They did. Oh, and, too, there was lots of— oh, what do they

speaker: NYE
words: call them— they've all quit here anymore—well, I can't think of it

speaker: NYE
words: now. We had a preacher lived across the street here, was one of 'em.

speaker: NYE
words: You could hear him preach, he preached in the Odd Fellows Hall down

speaker: NYE
words: there— you could sit here and listen to him, he talked so loud you

speaker: NYE
words: could hear him here. His name was Hoskins. (Chuckles) Oh, this town

speaker: NYE
words: had plenty of preachers! Plenty of churches, too. And the German

speaker: NYE
words: Lutherans had one up there on the corner, and they preached in German.

speaker: NYE
words: And during the war they made 'em quit. We didn't have any Swedes

speaker: NYE
words: here but we had Germans.

speaker: SS
words: What do you think it was like for German people during the first war?

speaker: SS
words: Do you think they were kind of a little scared about what might hap

speaker: SS
words: pen to them?

speaker: NYE
words: I don't think they were scared, but they sure wanted everything for

speaker: NYE
words: theirselves. You know. They wasn't for the United States in, the

speaker: NYE
words: war, they was for theirself.

speaker: SS
words: I guess they stopped teaching German in a lot of places. In schools

speaker: SS
words: they stopped teaching. You couldn't teach it.in schools.

speaker: NYE
words: Yeah. They had a German school at And they shut it. But

speaker: NYE
words: I lived next door to an old German woman and I learned to talk German

speaker: NYE
words: when I was little. Then when I went to school, at Walla Walla I took

speaker: NYE
words: German, you know. Then when I taught school at Big Bend I had to

speaker: NYE
words: know it, because that's all they ever talked over there. So it's a

speaker: NYE
words: good thing I learned it, wasn't it? (Chuckles)

speaker: SS
words: Yes. I didn't know there were so many German: families over there.

speaker: NYE
words: Oh, yeah. German— Russian German.

speaker: SS
words: Oh, that's right!

speaker: NYE
words: You see, in the early days, they give every other section of land to

speaker: NYE
words: the government to homestead. Well, the Russian-Germans come and took

speaker: NYE
words: it up. This town had lots of Germans.

speaker: SS
words: I think about this Foster— were there many people who died in town

speaker: SS
words: who weren't cured because they were too far gone? And then probably

speaker: SS
words: your father buried some of them, too.

speaker: NYE
words: Oh, yes. Yes, because these roominghouses. I remember, they were full

speaker: NYE
words: of patients, and some of 'em were bedfast and some of 'em were crip

speaker: NYE
words: pled, you know. Some of 'em had different things than cancer.

speaker: SS
words: I wonder if there were many jobs then for young women, or older women

speaker: SS
words: to be nurses when the hospital was here?

speaker: NYE
words: I don't remember. Now, Foster's wife was a nurse. And I'll tell you

speaker: NYE
words: something about her and you can take it or leave it or think I'm a

speaker: NYE
words: liar!! They lived in that old big house up there, and there was a

speaker: NYE
words: window that opened onto the porch, there was kind of a round thing,

speaker: NYE
words: and as long as that window was in that house, you could stand out in

speaker: NYE
words: the street and look up there and see her in that window. That's so!

speaker: NYE
words: And the people that bought it, Greens, they moved there after Foster

speaker: NYE
words: left, and they had the window taken out and different glass put in

speaker: NYE
words: and it didn't do a bit of good! She stood right there! (Laughter)

speaker: NYE
words: That's the truth!

speaker: SS
words: Could most anybody in town see her?

speaker: NYE
words: Why sure. Anybody could see it that looked!

speaker: SS
words: Boy!! Huh!!

speaker: NYE
words: It got to be just commonplace, nobody thought nothing about it!!

speaker: NYE
words: But Greens took the glass out and put a new one in and it didn't do

speaker: NYE
words: any good!!

speaker: SS
words: I heard a story about— now, I never got it right— but it was kind

speaker: SS
words: of garbled the way I heard it, but it had to do with a baby getting

speaker: SS
words: killed and something to do with a nurse; maybe the nurse was killing

speaker: SS
words: the baby or something. Was that there?

speaker: NYE
words: No. I never heard that.

speaker: SS
words: Maybe that was in Kendrick.

speaker: NYE
words: I never heard it. No. Maybe it was.

speaker: SS
words: But nothing about a baby who died, or got killed?

speaker: NYE
words: No.

speaker: SS
words: That must be something else. Well, what about— Mrs. Agers said that

speaker: SS
words: I had to ask you to tell me the story about the big, fat man that

speaker: SS
words: hated Juliaette.

speaker: NYE
words: (Laughs) Yeah, Old John Gorman. He homesteaded all that land that

speaker: NYE
words: lays up there on this side of the hill, you know. At one time, long

speaker: NYE
words: after he died they had it all platted off and was going to build a

speaker: NYE
words: asylum there and then they didn't. But anyway, this John Gorman homesteaded

speaker: NYE
words: all of that and this town had a lot of people that was spi

speaker: NYE
words: ritualists. You know, the Snyders, they named the town after Juliaetta,

speaker: NYE
words: their folks all spiritualists.

speaker: SS
words: Now, what are spiritualists? I've heard of that.

speaker: NYE
words: Well, it's a kind of a religion, I guess, I don't know. But they

speaker: NYE
words: believe in having seanCes and seeing all these things, you know. And

speaker: NYE
words: so they had a big sean£e at one time and all of 'em set together,

speaker: NYE
words: and the spirits told 'em to dig a coal mine over here on top of the hill

speaker: NYE
words: so they did. And they dug on it for years. They said there'd be

speaker: NYE
words: coal, but there was no coal there. Never was. But this Old Gorman,

speaker: NYE
